description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline using the global image registry.gitlab.com/fdroid/ci-images-server:latest and overriding images where needed. Define a test stage with three jobs: check_git_repos (runs on schedules, uses debian:buster-slim, runs apt-get update; apt-get -qy install --no-install-recommends ca-certificates git python3-yaml; tools/check-git-repo-availability.py || export EXITVALUE=1; test -d public || mkdir public; cp `git status | grep -Eo 'metadata/.*\.yml'` public/ || true; exit $EXITVALUE; uploads the public/ directory as an artifact on failure, expiring in 1 month), fdroid-buildserver (runs only on the DONOTRUN branch, caches build and unsigned directories with key $CI_JOB_NAME, produces artifacts named ${CI_PROJECT_PATH}_${CI_JOB_STAGE}_${CI_COMMIT_REF_NAME}_${CI_COMMIT_SHA} containing json, libscout-logs, logs, stats, unsigned, always kept for 1 week, and executes virsh destroy/undefine, git resets, fdroid build, Java LibScout analysis, and JSON pretty‑print commands), and lint (uses the global image, prepares a Debian stretch‑backports repository in before_script, installs fdroidserver, then in script fetches fdroiddata if not in the fdroid namespace, determines changed app IDs, runs fdroid lint with error handling, strips EXIF from images, runs several metadata checks, and exits with the collected status). Add a deploy stage with a pages job (runs on schedules, uses ruby:2.3, caches vendor/ruby, removes any existing public directory, installs git_stats gem, generates the site with git_stats generate --silent --out-path=public, and publishes the public/ directory as a GitLab Pages artifact).
